spec_match.rationale: The page starts a real tab capture, binds the preview tile with `CaptureController.prototype.forwardWheel`, and logs trusted wheel activity against that bound element.

interactive.rationale: Users can start and stop capture, inspect capability and binding state, wheel over the live preview tile, and see fallback or forwarding telemetry in the action log.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index promises wheel passthrough from the preview tile rather than synthetic buttons, and the page demonstrates that exact video-conferencing flow.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now covers unsupported browsers, permission/binding rejection, and source-ended cleanup, but it still does not instrument a captured page to prove how huge wheel deltas are clamped at the receiver.

references.rationale: The `see also` section cites both the ChromeStatus entry and the Captured Surface Control draft.

design_system.rationale: Local styling uses shared CSS variables, keeps button background and text color paired, and includes the required origin-trial/flag banner.

openQuestions.title: Show wheel delta clamping / translation
openQuestions.detail: The demo logs trusted wheel deltas observed over the preview tile, but it does not include an instrumented captured page that can report the actual scroll delta received after the browser applies any clamping or translation. Add that round-trip only if this concept needs deeper receiver-side evidence.

summary: The required origin-trial/flag banner is now visible before the demo, unsupported browsers degrade through the capability probe, and the source-ended path tears down the capture with an action-log entry. The only remaining follow-up is optional deeper instrumentation for receiver-side wheel delta clamping.
